zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William de Colville - Fourth Lord of Oxnam於1225年出生在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德時，他的父親John de Colville - Third Laird of Oxnam31歲，母親Marshall32歲。他和Joan De Normanville至少有2個兒子和4個女兒。他去世於1280年在英國蘇格蘭享年55歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Colville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Colleville in Seine-Maritime, France, named with the Scandinavian personal name Koli (see Cole 2) + Old French ville ‘settlement, village’. The Scottish branch of the family were descended from Philip de Colville, who came to Scotland as a household knight of kings Malcolm IV (1153–65) and William ‘the Lion’ (1165–1214).

variants.name: Cole
meanings: English: usually from the Middle English and Old French personal name Col(e), Coll(e), Coul(e), a pet form of Nicol (see Nichol and Nicholas ), a common personal name from the mid 13th century onward. English families with this name migrated to Scotland and to Ulster (especially Fermanagh).

English: occasionally perhaps from a different (early) Middle English personal name Col, of native English or Scandinavian origin. Old English Cola was originally a nickname from Old English col ‘coal’ in the sense ‘coal-black (of hair), swarthy’ and is the probable source of most of the examples in Domesday Book. In the northern and eastern counties of England settled by Vikings in the 10th and 11th centuries, alternative sources are Old Norse Kolr and Koli (either from a nickname ‘the swarthy one’ or a short form of names in Kol-), and Old Norse Kollr (from a nickname, perhaps ‘the bald one’).
English: nickname for someone with swarthy skin or black hair, from Middle English col, coul(e) ‘charcoal, coal’ (Old English col).

variants.name: Colvin
meanings: Scottish and Irish (Donegal, Antrim): variant of   Colville  , probably reflecting a local pronunciation. The name was taken to Ulster in the 17th century.
Manx: if not identical with 1, perhaps from the Old Norse personal name Kolbeinn, with /v/ substituted for /b/.

English: from the rare Middle English personal name Colwin, Colvin, which may be a borrowing into English of a Welsh name whose modern form is Collwyn ‘white’, or of colwyn ‘doe, puppy, pet dog’, or of the placename Colwyn (Denbighshire, Radnorshire).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William de Colville - Fourth Lord of Oxnam was born in 1225, in Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, his father, John de Colville - Third Laird of Oxnam, was 31 and his mother, Marshall, was 32. He had at least 2 sons and 4 daughters with Joan De Normanville. He died in 1280, in Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 55.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William de Colville - Fourth Lord of Oxnam est né en 1225, à Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John de Colville - Third Laird of Oxnam, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Marshall, avait 32 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles avec Joan De Normanville. Il est décédé en 1280, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 55 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William de Colville - Fourth Lord of Oxnam(이)가 1225년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Colville - Third Laird of Oxnam(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Marshall(은)는 32세였다. 그는 Joan De Normanville(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1280년 영국 스코틀랜드에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William de Colville - Fourth Lord of Oxnam родился в 1225 г. в местности нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John de Colville - Third Laird of Oxnam было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Marshall, 32.
